reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The source document consists of 27 pages. The JSON output explicitly indicates that 19 sections were omitted (likely corresponding to pages 5-18, 20-22, 23, and 25). These pages contain significant content including broadband service inventory maps, adoption rates, barriers to deployment, and specific grant details that are not present in the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document contains 27 pages of information, including maps (Pages 5, 11), barriers to adoption (Pages 10, 12), and state vision/plan details (Pages 14-18).
concerns.location: Sections list, between elements 15 and 16 (index after section 4)

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There are two major omissions within sections that are otherwise present. First, Section 1 (Page 1) is missing the main presentation title 'What is Broadband?'. Second, Section 4 (Page 19) provides a table of regions but fails to include the accompanying contact names, phone numbers, and email addresses for the regional experts (Keith Conover, Jim Corrin, George T. Collier) which constitutes the primary data of that page.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 19: 'West Keith Conover 828-450-7933 Keith.Conover@nc.gov', 'Central Jim Corrin 919-353-1126 Jim.Corrin@nc.gov', 'East George T. Collier 919-754-6558 George.Collier@nc.gov'
concerns.output_quote: The table in Section 4 only contains the region names (West, Central, East) without the corresponding contact details.

concerns.analysis: The table on page 3 ('Broadband Speeds') is structurally corrupted in the output. The header row defines a two-column structure ('Activity' and 'Minimum Speed'). However, the rows serving as category headers in the original PDF (e.g., 'General Usage', 'Watching Video') are rendered in the JSON as rows containing only a single cell. This creates a column count mismatch within the table object, breaking its integrity for screen readers.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table on page 3 containing categories like 'General Usage', 'Watching Video', 'Video Conferencing', and 'Gaming'.
concerns.output_quote: In section 3, the table rows corresponding to these categories (indices 1, 9, 13, 17 in the rows array) only contain one 'cells' object, unlike the other rows which contain two.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output significantly changes the visual organization of several structured pages. Diagrams used to show relationships and process flow in the source are collapsed into simple lists. For example, on page 23, a flowchart with large arrows is converted into a series of bulleted text blocks. Similarly, the hexagonal groupings for 'Common Barriers' on pages 10 and 12 and the tiled layout for 'The Playbook' on page 21 are lost, fundamentally changing how the information is visually presented
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 23 shows 'Addressing the Homework Gap through Libraries' as a flowchart with distinct boxed sections connected by large downward arrows.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML renders the content of page 23 as a vertical stack of bulleted lists without any flowchart or diagrammatic structure.
concerns.location: Addressing the Homework Gap through Libraries (Page 23)

concerns.analysis: Several critical and meaningful images are missing from the HTML output. This includes multiple maps of North Carolina (Broadband Service Inventory on p. 5, Adoption Rates on p. 11, and Technical Assistance Regions on p. 19). Additionally, the infographic sequence of house icons (pages 6-9), the images for 'The Playbook' tiles (p. 21), and the report cover image (p. 22) are absent. These graphics provide essential context and data visualization that is not fully captured by the remaining text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 5 shows the 'North Carolina Broadband Service Inventory' map showing coverage areas across the state.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML renders the title and legend text for the map, but the map image itself is missing.

concerns.analysis: On page 2 ('Broadband Technology Types'), the original document contains a detailed multi-level bulleted list. The output merges this entire list into a single paragraph element, losing the structural distinction between the list items and sub-items.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2 bulleted list starting with 'Digital Subscriber Line (DSL)' through 'Satellite'.
concerns.output_quote: "- Digital Subscriber Line (DSL)\n  - Runs over traditional copper phone lines..."

concerns.analysis: Several pages in the source PDF contain clearly visible URLs and email addresses (e.g., pages 3, 4, 16, 26, 27). While the text for these links is preserved in the output, they have not been converted into functional link elements (hyperlinks), appearing only as plain text within paragraphs.

action_items: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.
